# SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Question A1

**[KTU University Exam – May 2024]**
**Q: Define a Constant-K filter. Why is it called "Constant-K"?**

**Model Answer:**
A Constant-K filter is a reactive ladder network designed using the image parameter method, in which the product of the series arm impedance ($Z_1$) and the shunt arm impedance ($Z_2$) is a **constant**, real, and frequency-independent value, $K^2$. Mathematically:

$$Z_1 \cdot Z_2 = K^2 = \frac{L}{C} = R_0^2$$

It is called *Constant-K* because $K$ remains the same (constant) for all operating frequencies — the $\omega$-dependence of $Z_1$ and $Z_2$ cancels out in their product. For a low-pass realization, $Z_1 = j\omega L$ (series inductor) and $Z_2 = 1/(j\omega C)$ (shunt capacitor).

---

### Question A2

**[KTU University Exam – Dec 2023]**
**Q: Write the expressions for characteristic impedance of T and $\pi$ sections of a Constant-K Low Pass Filter.**

**Model Answer:**
The characteristic (image) impedances for the two standard sections in the **pass-band** are:

$$Z_{0T} = R_0 \sqrt{1 - \left(\frac{f}{f_c}\right)^2}$$

$$Z_{0\pi} = \frac{R_0}{\sqrt{1 - \left(\frac{f}{f_c}\right)^2}}$$

where $R_0 = \sqrt{L/C}$ is the design resistance and $f_c = 1/(\pi\sqrt{LC})$ is the cutoff frequency.

---

## 5.2 Part B Questions (14 Marks Each — Internal Choice)

### Question A (14 Marks)

**[KTU University Exam – May 2024, Module 4, Choice A]**

**Q: Design a Constant-K Low Pass Filter with cutoff frequency $f_c = 1 \text{ kHz}$ and design resistance $R_0 = 500 \text{ }\Omega$.**
- **(a)** [7 Marks] Determine the values of $L$ and $C$, and draw both T and $\pi$ full-section circuits with component values.
- **(b)** [7 Marks] Calculate the characteristic impedance of the T-section at $f = 800 \text{ Hz}$, and the attenuation (in dB) at $f = 2 \text{ kHz}$.

#### Model Solution

**(a) [7 Marks]**

**Step 1:** Apply the design formula for inductance:

$$L = \frac{R_0}{\pi f_c} = \frac{500}{\pi \times 1000} = \frac{500}{3141.59} \approx 159.15 \text{ mH}$$

**Step 2:** Apply the design formula for capacitance:

$$C = \frac{1}{\pi f_c R_0} = \frac{1}{\pi \times 1000 \times 500} = \frac{1}{1.5708 \times 10^6} \approx 0.6366 \text{ }\mu\text{F}$$

**Step 3:** Construct T-section:

- Series arms: $L/2 = 79.58 \text{ mH}$ each
- Shunt arm: $C = 0.6366 \text{ }\mu\text{F}$

**Step 4:** Construct $\pi$-section:

- Series arm: $L = 159.15 \text{ mH}$
- Shunt arms: $2C = 1.273 \text{ }\mu\text{F}$ each

**(b) [7 Marks]**

**Step 1:** Compute $f/f_c$ at $f = 800 \text{ Hz}$:

$$\frac{f}{f_c} = \frac{800}{1000} = 0.8$$

**Step 2:** Calculate $Z_{0T}$:

$$Z_{0T} = R_0 \sqrt{1 - (0.8)^2} = 500 \sqrt{1 - 0.64} = 500 \sqrt{0.36} = 500 \times 0.6 = 300 \text{ }\Omega$$

**Step 3:** For attenuation at $f = 2 \text{ kHz}$, compute the frequency ratio:

$$\frac{f}{f_c} = \frac{2000}{1000} = 2.0$$

**Step 4:** Since $f > f_c$, we are in the stop-band. Apply attenuation formula:

$$\alpha = \cosh^{-1}(2) = \ln(2 + \sqrt{4-1}) = \ln(2 + \sqrt{3}) = \ln(3.732) \approx 1.317 \text{ Nepers}$$

**Step 5:** Convert to dB:

$$\alpha_{dB} = 8.686 \times 1.317 \approx 11.44 \text{ dB}$$

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Pitfall Warning:** Many students mistakenly use $\sin^{-1}$ or $\cos^{-1}$ in the **stop-band**, which is the pass-band formula! Remember: **$\beta = \cos^{-1}(f/f_c)$ for $f < f_c$** and **$\alpha = \cosh^{-1}(f/f_c)$ for $f > f_c$**. Wrong choice of trigonometric function costs **3 full marks**.

---

### Question B (14 Marks)

**[KTU University Exam – Dec 2023, Module 4, Choice B]**

**Q: For a Constant-K Low Pass Filter, derive the expressions for cutoff frequency and design resistance. Given $L = 100 \text{ mH}$ and $C = 4 \text{ }\mu\text{F}$, find:**
- **(a)** [7 Marks] The cutoff frequency $f_c$ and design resistance $R_0$.
- **(b)** [7 Marks] The values of components for T and $\pi$ full-sections, and the characteristic impedance at $f = 0.5 f_c$.

#### Model Solution

**(a) [7 Marks]**

**Step 1:** Apply the cutoff frequency formula:

$$f_c = \frac{1}{\pi \sqrt{LC}} = \frac{1}{\pi \sqrt{(100 \times 10^{-3})(4 \times 10^{-6})}}$$

**Step 2:** Compute the product:

$$LC = (0.1)(4 \times 10^{-6}) = 4 \times 10^{-7}$$

$$\sqrt{LC} = \sqrt{4 \times 10^{-7}} = 6.3246 \times 10^{-4}$$

**Step 3:** Final cutoff frequency:

$$f_c = \frac{1}{\pi \times 6.3246 \times 10^{-4}} = \frac{1}{1.987 \times 10^{-3}} \approx 503.3 \text{ Hz}$$

**Step 4:** Compute design resistance:

$$R_0 = \sqrt{\frac{L}{C}} = \sqrt{\frac{100 \times 10^{-3}}{4 \times 10^{-6}}} = \sqrt{25000} = 158.11 \text{ }\Omega$$

**(b) [7 Marks]**

**Step 1:** T-section values:

$$L_T = \frac{L}{2} = 50 \text{ mH} \quad ; \quad C_T = C = 4 \text{ }\mu\text{F}$$

**Step 2:** $\pi$-section values:

$$L_\pi = L = 100 \text{ mH} \quad ; \quad C_\pi = 2C = 8 \text{ }\mu\text{F}$$

**Step 3:** At $f = 0.5 f_c$, the ratio $f/f_c = 0.5$:

$$Z_{0T} = R_0 \sqrt{1 - (0.5)^2} = 158.11 \sqrt{1 - 0.25} = 158.11 \sqrt{0.75} = 158.11 \times 0.866$$

$$Z_{0T} \approx 136.93 \text{ }\Omega$$

**Step 4:** For $\pi$-section at same frequency:

$$Z_{0\pi} = \frac{R_0}{\sqrt{1 - (0.5)^2}} = \frac{158.11}{0.866} \approx 182.58 \text{ }\Omega$$

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Pitfall Warning:** A frequent mistake is writing the **shunt capacitance** for $\pi$-section as $C$ instead of $2C$. The mnemonic: *"T-section splits the L, $\pi$-section splits the C"*. Getting this wrong loses **2 marks** in design questions. Similarly, do NOT swap — T has $L/2$ (split inductor), not $2L$.

---

## 5.3 Topic Recap & Rapid-Revision Checklist

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **The 15 Things You MUST Remember for KTU ESE — Module 4, Constant-K LPF:**

- ☐ **1.** $Z_1 Z_2 = K^2 = L/C = R_0^2$ — the defining equation of Constant-K filters.
- ☐ **2.** $f_c = 1/(\pi\sqrt{LC})$ — cutoff frequency in **Hz** (most-tested formula).
- ☐ **3.** $\omega_c = 2/\sqrt{LC}$ — cutoff frequency in **rad/s**.
- ☐ **4.** $L = R_0/(\pi f_c)$ and $C = 1/(\pi f_c R_0)$ — the two **design equations** to memorize cold.
- ☐ **5.** T-section uses $L/2$ in series, $C$ in shunt — *"split the L"*.
- ☐ **6.** $\pi$-section uses $L$ in series, $2C$ in shunt — *"split the C"*.
- ☐ **7.** $Z_{0T} = R_0\sqrt{1 - (f/f_c)^2}$ — T-section image impedance.
- ☐ **8.** $Z_{0\pi} = R_0/\sqrt{1 - (f/f_c)^2}$ — $\pi$-section image impedance.
- ☐ **9.** Pass-band ($f < f_c$): $\alpha = 0$, signal passes with only phase shift.
- ☐ **10.** Stop-band ($f > f_c$): $\alpha = \cosh^{-1}(f/f_c)$ Nepers, attenuation only.
- ☐ **11.** $\beta = \cos^{-1}(f/f_c)$ rad in pass-band — phase constant, not attenuation.
- ☐ **12.** $\alpha_{dB} = 8.686 \cdot \alpha$ — conversion from Nepers to decibels.
- ☐ **13.** At $f = 0$: $X_L = 0$ (inductor → short), $X_C = \infty$ (capacitor → open) — explains why DC/low-f passes.
- ☐ **14.** At $f \to \infty$: $X_L = \infty$ (inductor → open), $X_C = 0$ (capacitor → short to GND) — explains why high-f is blocked.
- ☐ **15.** Constant-K filters are foundational but have a sharp corner at $f_c$ (not infinitely steep roll-off) — this limitation motivates the **m-derived filter** (covered later in Module 4).

> **Final Exam Tip:** Whenever you see "design" in a KTU question, immediately write down the **two design equations** ($L$ and $C$ formulas), substitute the given $R_0$ and $f_c$, and present results with proper units. This guarantees at least **10 of 14 marks**. The remaining 4 marks come from drawing the circuit correctly with $L/2$, $2C$ splits.
